title.narrative: Digital Rights: Improving Human Rights Online in Jordan

description.narrative: Internet users and civil society organizations (CSOs) that require additional protection: 1.women and minors. 2.persons at risk for their political activities, e.g. human rights defenders, bloggers and activists. 3.sexual and religious minorities.

Awareness​: To promote human rights through online outlets, focusing on raising broad-based awareness on digital risks and best practices for the protection of internet users and their rights, including activists and CSOs. Capacity​: To increase adoption of privacy enhancing tools, build capacities for digital resilience through innovative approaches and to support governmental efforts in digital rights. Help​: To provide incident response, digital security advice, and implementation support for CSOs and individuals at risk around Jordan. Monitoring​: Enhance knowledge, practice and partnerships for digital rights. Network and Localization​: Enhance sustainability of effort, response and advocacy for digital rights in Jordan.

mailing-address.narrative: Building 21, King Hussein Business Park, Al-Jandaweel, Amman 11181, Jordan.
Building 21, King Hussein Business Park, Al-Jandaweel, Amman 11181, Jordan.
Building 21, King Hussein Business Park, Al-Jandaweel, Amman 11181, Jordan.

description.narrative: Social Security for Finance and Admin Officer

receiver-org.narrative: Social Security for Finance and Admin Officer

description.narrative: Training of more than 40 journalists and media practitioners to cover digital rights issues.
Number of journalist and media practitioners who have received training on covering digital rights topics on media outlets.
Training sessions provided to journalists and media practitioners to cover digital rights issues, each training consists of five modules and is delivered in a five-day timespan.
Number of public panel events on digital rights issues with speakers of various stakeholder groups.
Number of public panels related to digital rights in which JOSA organized or participated.

Worked at length to establish a digital helpline process for the team to respond to cases in a professional manner. The process outlines exactly the steps to be taken by JOSA responders once they receive a case (in the same way that when you call 911, the first question they ask is your location, then proceed to ask other questions). This workflow is a constant work in progress, we’re leveraging our partnership with AccessNow’s helpline team (they respond to digital safety cases of human rights defenders on a global level). We hope that through this partnership we will build our team’s knowledge and capacity in emergency response. We have also launched a minimum viable product of our emergency response portal: http://er.jordanopensource.org/. The portal includes an explanation of ways we can help, what makes us trustworthy, and ways to contact us. Individuals can contact us via: a hotline number, our email address.
Establishing a trained rapid response team ready to support at risk individuals and organizations
Provide digital security assistance to more than 150+ at-risk individuals and organizations.

Started gathering interest from people wanting to join our CryptoJOSA team. We have started to identify and invite speakers for CryptoParties however they were delayed due to restrictions brought on by COVID-19. Potential speakers include: a contributor to the DP3T protocol for private proximity tracing and a core developer of SecureDrop a platform for whistleblowers to communicate anonymously with news organisations.

title.narrative: CryptoJOSA: Activities and community to promote privacy-enhancing technologies.

description.narrative: Started to identify and invite speakers for CryptoParties however they were delayed due to restrictions brought on by COVID-19. Potential speakers include: a contributor to the DP3T protocol for private proximity tracing and a core developer of SecureDrop a platform for whistleblowers to communicate anonymously with news organisations.
Public activities to promote privacy-enhancing technologies and the right to online privacy to activists, HRDs, journalists and the general public.
Publication of research findings related to local digital rights issues, as reports, policy recommendations and other forms of publications.
Output in the form of a technical report or policy paper
Participation in public consultations by the Ministry of Digital Economy and Entrepreneurship regarding ICT policies.

comment.narrative: Participation in public consultations by the ministry of digital economy and entrepreneurship regarding ICT policies

title.narrative: Supporting governmental efforts in digital rights

description.narrative: Continuous support to the Ministry of Digital Economy and Entrepreneurship when it comes to digital rights in Jordan. Drafting the Open Data Quality Framework; the framework outlays a systematic method to assess the viability of publishing datasets on the open government data portal and rate their quality. The ministry shared all important considerations for the framework, and JOSA took charge of redacting the Open Government Data Quality Framework. In such cases, JOSA is placed in a very good position to affect public policy in accordance with global digital rights principles. Providing 3 workshops on Open Data to over 65 government bodies, to help them start publishing open datasets. Within these workshops, we explained what is open data and its projected benefits. Also, we offered some exercises related to metadata and open formats and concluded the workshops with important considerations for open data quality.
Workshops to promote digital rights and their related tools/techniques in public entities.

title.narrative: Drafting and publishing of at least 3 frameworks/procedures/standards to be implemented by public administration.

description.narrative: Provide legal and technical assistance for the implementation of governmental standards, frameworks and policies to allow citizens free and participatory information access.
Number of meetings, roundtables with the government bodies in relation to support digital rights policies.

Identified civil society organisations to target in the next year. We also worked extensively on adapting the SafeTag digital security auditing framework to the local context. SafeTag is a framework specific to CSOs and so we saw a great benefit in adopting it. We also selected another framework called CyberStar which we will use for smaller audits, specifically CSOs with minimal IT infrastructure. We also ran mock audits on both frameworks in order to ensure the team is ready come year 2.
Creation and localization of digital security assessment framework to be implemented for local CSOs.
Number of civil society organizations of which has been digital security audited by JOSA.

title.narrative: Promotion of digital rights through online outlets

description.narrative: # of Infographics, # of social media posts, # of blog articles Raising awareness of digital rights via publishing blog posts, infographics for social media, and localising material related to digital rights.
Copywriting and graphic design of social media posts including video infographics to raise awareness of digitalrights topics
Creation of an ‘editorial team’ of tech-related bloggers and writers to promote digital rights
Localize and translate materials to be used as reference for awareness-raising campaigns, issuing alerts and guidance.
Publication of +100 online materials, like infographics, social media posts and blog articles.
